key_insights.insight: Founders possess a deep passion and intimate knowledge of their company that professional managers often lack, due to the 'biological parent' connection, permission to make radical changes, and having built the company from the ground up.
key_insights.quote: A founder brings three things that a professional manager doesn't have. The first thing a founder has is they're the biological parent...The second thing a founder has is they have the permission...And the third thing that a founder brings is you built it.

key_insights.insight: True company culture is not defined by written values, but by the consistent behaviors of its leaders and the shared instincts that develop over time.
key_insights.quote: Your culture is the behaviors of the leaders that get mimicked all the way down every single person...It's by basically leading by example every single day and taking a survey of every single thing happening and constantly shaping it, pruning it like a gardener.

key_insights.insight: The most valuable asset a company has is its culture: the unique way it approaches problems and executes solutions, as this is difficult for competitors to replicate.
key_insights.quote: Culture is something that people learn to develop these shared instincts and it's so important because it's your ultimate intellectual property, not your technology, not your recipes, not your exclusive contract vendor relationships.

key_insights.insight: Entrepreneurs should possess not only the vision to see a different future, but also the ability to inspire and lead others through challenges and setbacks.
key_insights.quote: They have the audacity to believe that they can do it and they have the ability to convince other people to go on that journey with them.

key_insights.insight: Founders should design the culture they want rather than allowing it to organically emerge, since an unmanaged culture may not be desirable.
key_insights.quote: You must design the culture you want. Otherwise, it will be designed for you and you might not like what emerges.

key_insights.insight: Culture emerges when employees act the same way the founder would, even when the founder is not present.
key_insights.quote: The moment I cannot be in the room and the same action happens as if I was in the room, that's the moment it goes from management to culture.

actionable_advice.advice: Actively shape and prune your company's culture like a gardener, rather than letting it evolve without guidance.
actionable_advice.how_to: Constantly observe what's happening, identify behaviors that align with the desired culture, reinforce them, and correct those that don't, leading by example.

actionable_advice.advice: Design the people and culture function to bring out the best in employees.
actionable_advice.how_to: Reimagine HR as a strategic function focused on people and culture instead of administration.

memorable_stories: Brian shares an early rejection email from an investor who didn't believe in the potential of Airbnb's market.
Brian tells a story about ascending a mountain to illustrate the founder's journey and ability to inspire others to overcome obstacles.
contrarian_claims: Company culture is not built by writing out values or principles and displaying them; culture is built by the actions of leaders and mimicked down the line.
HR should not be an administrative function but should strategically be focused on people and culture.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - CEO Diaries: Airbnb’s Founder Brian Chesky on Brutal Rejection, Great Leadership, and The Biggest Mistake Founders Make!
